headline: The Deportation That Broke Three Laws
context: On 9 March 2026, Malaysia deported 12 asylum seekers despite an active High Court injunction and valid UNHCR documentation.

cards.big: Twelve people with court protection were deported anyway.
cards.sub: High Court stay: 7 March. Deportation: 9 March.
The AG's Chambers has not explained the sequence.

cards.big: All 12 held valid UNHCR refugee documentation.
cards.sub: Malaysia cooperates with UNHCR case by case. This was not one of those cases.

cards.big: Fourth mass deportation challenged in court since 2021.
cards.sub: Courts can review detention. Enforcement compliance: inconsistent.

cards.big: If the executive can override a court order, what is a court order worth?
The deportation happened. The court did not prevent it. Draw your own conclusion.
